message:
chore(beads): Sync issue tracking state

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -15,6 +15,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-1gdn","title":"Add lipgloss individual border edge colors and Transform method","description":"Add per-edge border colors and Transform method to Lipgloss.

Scope:
- Extend border style API to set color per edge (top/right/bottom/left).
- Implement Transform method for style manipulation consistent with Go (e.g., normalize or mutate styles).
- Add unit tests for per-edge colors and Transform behavior.
- Update docs if API changes are user-visible.","acceptance_criteria":"Criteria:
- Per-edge border colors render correctly in multiple combinations.
- Transform method behaves as documented and has unit coverage.
- Conformance fixtures updated if needed to include per-edge color scenarios.","notes":"Files: crates/lipgloss/src/style.rs, border.rs, renderer.rs. Maintain backward compatibility where practical (but no shims).","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-25T01:09:48.923880193Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T18:15:01.451539959Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T18:15:01.450257984Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-1i7e","title":"demo_showcase: CLI contract (modes, flags, help)","description":"Define the binary UX for launching the demo.

Proposed flags/subcommands:
- default: run interactive local TUI
- `--theme <name|auto>`
- `--no-animations`
- `--no-mouse`
- `--no-color` (force ASCII)
- `--seed <u64>` (stable demo data)
- `ssh --addr :2222 --host-key <path>` (optional; gated)
- `export --html <path>` (optional: export a static HTML snapshot of the UI)

Acceptance:
- `--help` is clean and self-explanatory; flags map directly to implementation toggles.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:07:30.810927082Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:05.884615335Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["build","cli","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1i7e","depends_on_id":"bd-33mv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:05.884573066Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1i7e","depends_on_id":"bd-szmb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:07:30.830556320Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1knu","title":"Quality: headless self-check mode (CI-friendly)","description":"Add a non-interactive mode, e.g. `demo_showcase --self-check`, that:
- builds initial app state
- renders one or more pages
- exits 0 on success

Why:
- Lets CI prove the demo compiles and can render without needing a TTY.

Acceptance:
- Works in `TERM=dumb` / no-tty environments.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:13:29.630984785Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:21:29.218655951Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1knu","depends_on_id":"bd-1i7e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:21:26.513764136Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1knu","depends_on_id":"bd-2zeq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:13:29.649380944Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1knu","depends_on_id":"bd-ab35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:21:29.218612410Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bd-1kx7","title":"E2E: extend CI script to run full scenario suite","description":"After the initial smoke script exists, extend it to run the full E2E suite (wizard, docs, and any future scenarios).

Acceptance:
- The script can optionally run:
  - smoke-only (fast)
  - full suite (slower, more coverage)
- CI can choose which profile to run based on context (PR vs nightly).","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:35:27.825203081Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:35:44.326468097Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","e2e","script","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1kx7","depends_on_id":"bd-3449","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:35:42.979497295Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1kx7","depends_on_id":"bd-3nrc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:35:41.602626554Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1kx7","depends_on_id":"bd-miqo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:35:44.326429546Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1kx7","depends_on_id":"bd-pfqw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:35:27.846212242Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1nwu","title":"Settings: toggles (mouse, animations, no-color, syntax)","description":"Expose runtime toggles:
- mouse on/off
- animations on/off
- force ASCII/no color
- syntax highlighting + line numbers (if enabled)

Implementation notes:
- This page should mutate the live app state, but also reflect the initial defaults from `demo_showcase::Config`.

Acceptance:
- Toggling updates app behavior live without restart.
- Toggles are discoverable and have immediate, visible effects (not hidden flags).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:01.250474646Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:29:20.250220945Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","settings"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1nwu","depends_on_id":"bd-274o","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:01.272885316Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1nwu","depends_on_id":"bd-2k35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:20:54.907924170Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1nwu","depends_on_id":"bd-2q95","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:28:38.554374326Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1nwu","depends_on_id":"bd-2szb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:21:00.214777601Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1nwu","depends_on_id":"bd-3s4n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:21:02.860339836Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1o6y","title":"Implement huh Multiselect, Text, FilePicker, Note field types","description":"Implement missing Huh field types: Multiselect, FilePicker, and Note. (Textarea is tracked separately under bd-212m.4.1.)

Scope:
- Add field structs + state for multiselect, filepicker, note in crates/huh/src/lib.rs.
- Implement update/view logic and keybindings consistent with Go.
- Add validation hooks and theming support for each field.
- Expand conformance fixtures and add unit tests for each field type.","acceptance_criteria":"Criteria:
- Conformance fixtures for multiselect, text, filepicker, and note pass with no skips.
- Unit tests cover input handling, selection toggles, and rendering for each field.
- FEATURE_PARITY.md updated to reflect field coverage.","notes":"Textarea scope moved to bd-212m.4.1 to keep parity gap tracking focused.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-25T01:09:22.524453153Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T01:33:37.237925265Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T01:19:31.436260994Z","close_reason":"Implemented Text and FilePicker fields. MultiSelect and Note were already present. All field types have builder pattern, validation, keyboard handling, theme support, and view rendering.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-1oe0","title":"demo_showcase core: optionally use bubbletea derive macro for one model","description":"Optional: demonstrate the `bubbletea-macros` derive by using `#[derive(bubbletea::Model)]` for a simple model (e.g., a modal/palette).

Acceptance:
- Shows the macro ergonomics without forcing it everywhere.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:14:52.572059183Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:30.485993328Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bubbletea","demo_showcase","macros"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1oe0","depends_on_id":"bd-3j9x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:14:52.588543294Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1oe0","depends_on_id":"bd-cakm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:30.485956680Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
@@ -148,7 +149,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-3mh2","title":"demo_showcase theme: semantic tokens + shared style helpers","description":"Build the styling foundation for the entire app.

Implement:
- A theme module that maps lipgloss `ThemeContext` (ColorSlots) into semantic UI primitives.
- Shared helpers: `card()`, `panel()`, `badge()`, `button()`, `table_style()`, `modal()`, `status_bar()`, etc.

Guidelines:
- No ad-hoc hex strings scattered across pages.
- Prefer semantic roles (primary/success/warn/error/muted) and allow theme switching.

Acceptance:
- A page can be built using only shared style helpers + minimal page-specific overrides.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:23.252512028Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:32.937718717Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","lipgloss","theme"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3mh2","depends_on_id":"bd-2ucj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:32.937693109Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3mh2","depends_on_id":"bd-39zu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:23.270859361Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3mh2","depends_on_id":"bd-cakm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:31.724599289Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3mtt","title":"demo_showcase core: command palette (search + actions)","description":"Optional but very compelling polish feature.

Implement a command palette (think `/`) that lets you:
- jump to pages
- run actions (toggle theme, export view, start/stop job simulation)

Acceptance:
- Palette is keyboard-friendly, uses bubbles textinput/list, and feels fast.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:02.480166189Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:28.035124279Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3mtt","depends_on_id":"bd-14v4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:26.750440223Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3mtt","depends_on_id":"bd-1aad","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:28.035096998Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3mtt","depends_on_id":"bd-3j9x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:02.499012063Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3mxk","title":"Jobs: progress visualization polish","description":"Make progress visualization visually compelling.

Use:
- `bubbles::progress` + lipgloss styling
- spinners for indeterminate states
- color-coded statuses

Acceptance:
- Looks like a modern CI dashboard, not a toy.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:10:23.217085325Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:59.582392617Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","jobs","progress"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3mxk","depends_on_id":"bd-1dr6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:10:23.232965048Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3mxk","depends_on_id":"bd-3rwl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:56.844461854Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3mxk","depends_on_id":"bd-7jxu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:59.582343606Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-3nrc","title":"E2E: CLI script wrapper for CI + local debugging","description":"Add a human-friendly script that runs the E2E suite with good logs.

Deliverables:
- `scripts/demo_showcase_e2e.sh` (or similar) that:
  - sets deterministic env (seed, no-color optional)
  - runs `cargo test -p demo_showcase` with clear output
  - on failure, prints where artifacts were written

Acceptance:
- Running the script locally gives a fast, reliable signal and high-quality failure output.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:40.876265868Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:41.305627488Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","e2e","script","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3nrc","depends_on_id":"bd-299e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:41.305600278Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3nrc","depends_on_id":"bd-3449","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:38.743598434Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3nrc","depends_on_id":"bd-4d5e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:37.442689140Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3nrc","depends_on_id":"bd-miqo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:40.034641656Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3nrc","depends_on_id":"bd-pfqw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:40.895165789Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bd-3nrc","title":"E2E: CLI script wrapper for CI + local debugging","description":"Add a human-friendly script that runs the *smoke* E2E suite with good logs.

Deliverables:
- `scripts/demo_showcase_e2e.sh` (or similar) that:
  - sets deterministic env (seed, no-color optional)
  - runs `cargo test -p demo_showcase` with clear output
  - on failure, prints where artifacts were written

Notes:
- The full scenario suite is added by a follow-up task so we get early value.

Acceptance:
- Running the script locally gives a fast, reliable signal and high-quality failure output.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:40.876265868Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:35:56.036634053Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","e2e","script","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3nrc","depends_on_id":"bd-299e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:41.305600278Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3nrc","depends_on_id":"bd-4d5e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:37.442689140Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3nrc","depends_on_id":"bd-pfqw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:40.895165789Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3o6n","title":"QA and Stabilization Session: wish, lipgloss, glamour, huh","description":"Completed a deep dive review and fix cycle for the codebase.

Fixed:
- wish: Output flushing, bubbletea integration, input handling.
- lipgloss: Theme initialization bugs, missing error types.
- glamour: Table rendering (replaced naive implementation with table.rs), updated tests.
- huh: Doc examples, missing helper methods for value extraction.
- charmed_log: Date math (migrated to chrono).

Verified:
- All crates compile cleanly.
- Tests pass for all modified crates.
- Workspace check passes.

Closed related epics/tasks.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:46:22.301048189Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T23:46:29.525183754Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T23:46:29.525106669Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-3pm6","title":"demo_showcase data: action API (start job/deploy, emit logs/toasts)","description":"Create a clean *domain action API* so multiple UI surfaces can trigger the same behaviors without depending on each other.

Rationale:
- Currently Jobs page actions and Wizard completion both need to start jobs/deployments.
- If Wizard depends on Jobs page code (or vice versa), the architecture becomes tangled.

Deliverables:
- A set of pure-ish functions (or a small service struct) that:
  - create/start/cancel/retry jobs
  - start deployments / rollbacks
  - emit structured log entries
  - emit user-facing notifications (as app messages)
- The action layer should accept the current domain state + inputs and return:
  - updated state
  - side effects expressed as bubbletea `Cmd` / `AsyncCmd` (feature-gated)
  - events/messages for UI (toasts, navigation)

Acceptance:
- Jobs page and Wizard both call this API (no cross-dependencies between pages).
- The API is unit-testable (given state + input -> expected state/events).","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:30:09.840599209Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:30:28.941234528Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","data","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3pm6","depends_on_id":"bd-278q","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:30:09.860556382Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3pm6","depends_on_id":"bd-2hwq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:30:28.941199473Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3ps4","title":"Interactions: enable mouse mode + click/scroll mapping","description":"Turn on mouse support and map it to UX.

Implementation:
- Run Program with `with_mouse_cell_motion()` (or all motion if needed).
- Handle scroll wheel for viewports/lists/tables.
- Handle clicks to focus/select.

Acceptance:
- Mouse feels natural; doesn’t fight keyboard navigation.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:32.064610623Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:56.981846390Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","input","mouse"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ps4","depends_on_id":"bd-114p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:32.093132249Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ps4","depends_on_id":"bd-28jp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:56.981806786Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ps4","depends_on_id":"bd-2tl1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:55.722943138Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
